threat_model.name: Acme E-Commerce Platform v3.1 Week 2 -- STRIDE + MITRE ATT&CK

threats.title: Credential stuffing against Cognito authentication endpoint
threats.description: Attacker leverages credential databases from breached sites (e.g., Collection #1-5 with 2.2B records) to mass-authenticate against the Cognito user pool. Without WAF rate limiting (CNAPP-AWS-EC-007), the attacker can test thousands of credential pairs per minute against the /api/auth/login endpoint. Successful auth yields JWT tokens with access to payment methods, order history, and PII.
mitigations: Associate AWS WAF with ALB and enable AWSManagedRulesBotControlRuleSet
Enable Cognito advanced security features for risk-based adaptive authentication
Enforce MFA for all user accounts via Cognito user pool settings
Implement CAPTCHA challenge after 3 consecutive failed login attempts
Deploy credential breach detection using HaveIBeenPwned API integration

threats.title: JWT token forgery via weak signing secret
threats.description: If the JWT signing secret stored in Secrets Manager is compromised through the SSRF chain (CVE-2024-22259 + CNAPP-AWS-EC-002 over-privileged IAM role), an attacker can forge valid JWT tokens for any user including administrators. The forged token bypasses all authentication checks across all microservices that validate JWTs.
threats.mitre_technique: T1134.001 -- Access Token Manipulation: Token Impersonation/Theft
mitigations: Use RS256 asymmetric JWT signing instead of HS256 symmetric secret
Rotate JWT signing keys every 24 hours with grace period for validation
Implement JWT token binding to client IP and User-Agent fingerprint
Add short expiration (15 min access, 7 day refresh) with token revocation list in Redis

threats.title: Payment amount manipulation in inter-service transit
threats.description: Attacker who has compromised the Order Service (via deserialization CWE-502) modifies the payment amount in the internal API call from Order Service to Payment Service. Without HMAC-signed payloads between services, the Payment Service accepts the tampered amount. The attacker can set order totals to $0.01 while receiving full-price merchandise.
threats.mitre_technique: T1565.002 -- Data Manipulation: Transmitted Data Manipulation
mitigations: HMAC-SHA256 signature on payment request payloads using per-service signing keys
Server-side price recalculation from product catalog database in Payment Service
Mutual TLS between Order and Payment services with certificate pinning
Idempotency keys with server-side amount verification against original cart

threats.title: SQL injection leading to cardholder data extraction
threats.description: CVE-2024-1597 (CVSS 9.8) in PostgreSQL JDBC driver combined with the CWE-89 finding in UserController.java:42 creates a confirmed SQL injection path. The user-search microservice uses PreferQueryMode=SIMPLE which is the exact vulnerable configuration. Attacker injects UNION SELECT to extract tokenized card data, billing addresses, and transaction history from the payments schema.
mitigations: Upgrade postgresql JDBC to 42.7.3 immediately (P0)
Remove PreferQueryMode=SIMPLE from user-search connection string
Enforce parameterized queries via static analysis rule in CI/CD pipeline
Database-level: revoke SELECT on payments schema from user-search service account
Deploy AWS WAF SQLi rule set as defense-in-depth (CNAPP-AWS-EC-007)

threats.title: Order transaction audit log gaps enable fraud disputes
threats.description: CloudTrail data events are disabled for RDS and Lambda (CNAPP-AWS-EC-005). A malicious insider or compromised service can modify order records, refund transactions, or delete entries without a tamper-evident audit trail. Customer disputes cannot be resolved because there is no cryptographic proof of the original transaction state.
threats.mitre_technique: T1070.003 -- Indicator Removal: Clear Command History
mitigations: Enable CloudTrail data events for RDS and Lambda (fix CNAPP-AWS-EC-005)
Implement application-level audit log with immutable append-only storage
Use PostgreSQL row-level security to prevent UPDATE/DELETE on finalized orders
Ship audit logs to a separate AWS account with S3 Object Lock (WORM)

threats.title: Stack trace information leakage via error handler
threats.description: The global ErrorHandler.java (CWE-200 SARIF finding) returns full Java stack traces in HTTP 500 responses including Hibernate entity class names, PostgreSQL table schemas, internal service hostnames, and Spring Security filter chain details. This information accelerates all other attacks by revealing the exact technology stack, version numbers, and internal architecture.
threats.mitre_technique: T1592.004 -- Gather Victim Host Information: Client Configurations
mitigations: Replace detailed error responses with generic message and correlation ID
Configure Spring Boot error handling: server.error.include-stacktrace=never
Log full errors server-side to CloudWatch with structured JSON format
Add integration test that verifies no stack traces in 4xx/5xx responses

threats.title: PII exposure via mixed-classification S3 bucket
threats.description: The S3 media bucket (CNAPP-AWS-EC-001) stores both public product images and private customer invoice PDFs under the same bucket with Block Public Access disabled. An attacker can enumerate the /invoices/ prefix via ListBucket permission and download invoice PDFs containing customer names, addresses, email addresses, order totals, and partial card numbers.
mitigations: Separate media and invoices into distinct S3 buckets with different policies
Enable Block Public Access on the invoices bucket
Use pre-signed URLs with 15-minute expiration for invoice access
Enable S3 server-side encryption with KMS for the invoices bucket
Add CloudTrail S3 data events to detect unauthorized object access

threats.title: Application-layer DDoS via missing WAF
threats.description: Without AWS WAF (CNAPP-AWS-EC-007), the ALB forwards all traffic directly to ECS Fargate tasks. An attacker can launch a Layer 7 DDoS attack targeting expensive API endpoints (product search with complex queries, order processing). The auto-scaling group will scale up ECS tasks, but the RDS database connection pool (max 100 connections) becomes the bottleneck, causing cascading failures across all services.
threats.mitre_technique: T1499.003 -- Endpoint Denial of Service: Application Exhaustion Flood
mitigations: Deploy AWS WAF with rate-based rules (2000 req/5min per IP)
Enable AWS Shield Advanced for DDoS protection with 24/7 DRT support
Implement connection pooling with PgBouncer between ECS and RDS
Add circuit breaker pattern (Resilience4j) to prevent cascade failures
Cache product search results in ElastiCache with 60-second TTL

threats.title: Session cache poisoning via unauthenticated Redis access
threats.description: ElastiCache Redis (CNAPP-AWS-EC-008) has AUTH disabled. Any compromised service or EC2 instance in the VPC can connect to Redis port 6379 and execute FLUSHALL, destroying all user sessions and shopping carts simultaneously. Alternatively, an attacker can overwrite session tokens to hijack authenticated users, or inject malicious data into cached search results.
mitigations: Enable Redis AUTH with a strong token from Secrets Manager (fix CNAPP-AWS-EC-008)
Enable in-transit encryption (TLS) for all Redis connections
Restrict Redis security group to only ECS service security groups
Disable dangerous Redis commands (FLUSHALL, FLUSHDB, CONFIG, DEBUG) via parameter group
Implement session integrity checks with HMAC on serialized session data

threats.title: AWS account takeover via SSRF + over-privileged IAM role
threats.description: This is the highest-severity compound attack chain: (1) Attacker exploits CVE-2024-22259 SSRF in WebhookHandler.java to access EC2 metadata service at 169.254.169.254. (2) Retrieves temporary credentials for the ecommerce-api-role which has AdministratorAccess (CNAPP-AWS-EC-002). (3) Uses stolen credentials to create new IAM user with programmatic access. (4) Full AWS account takeover: access all S3 data, modify RDS, create backdoor Lambda functions, exfiltrate customer database.
mitigations: Replace AdministratorAccess with least-privilege policy (P0, fix CNAPP-AWS-EC-002)
Enable IMDSv2 (require hop limit = 1) on all ECS Fargate tasks
Fix SSRF vulnerability: URL scheme and hostname allowlist in WebhookHandler
Enable AWS GuardDuty for anomalous credential usage detection
Deploy VPC endpoints for AWS services to eliminate need for public internet egress

threats.title: Deserialization RCE escalation from Order Queue to database tier
threats.description: CWE-502 unsafe deserialization in OrderProcessor.java:89 processes messages from SQS order queue. An attacker who can inject a crafted serialized Java object into the queue (via compromised Order Service or direct SQS SendMessage) achieves remote code execution inside the Lambda function. The Lambda has IAM permissions to access the payments RDS database, enabling direct SQL queries to extract cardholder data.
threats.mitre_technique: T1059.004 -- Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
mitigations: Replace Java ObjectInputStream with Jackson JSON deserialization
Add JEP 290 ObjectInputFilter to allowlist only Order.class and its dependencies
Restrict SQS SendMessage permission to only the Order Service IAM role
Run Lambda with minimal IAM role: only rds-data:ExecuteStatement on payments DB
Deploy Lambda inside VPC to prevent outbound data exfiltration

threats.title: Container escape via root execution in ECS Fargate
threats.description: ECS task definitions run containers as root (CNAPP-AWS-EC-006). If an attacker achieves RCE via the deserialization vulnerability or SSTI in TemplateEngine.java (CWE-94), root access increases the blast radius. The attacker can modify container filesystem, install persistence mechanisms, and attempt Fargate isolation escapes via /proc/self/exe or shared kernel vulnerabilities.
mitigations: Set non-root user in ECS task definition (UID 1000:1000)
Update Dockerfiles: RUN addgroup --system app && adduser --system --ingroup app app; USER app
Enable ECS Exec audit logging to detect interactive container access
Drop all Linux capabilities except NET_BIND_SERVICE in task definition
Enable Fargate platform version 1.4.0+ with improved isolation

threats.title: Malicious image upload leading to server-side code execution
threats.description: The image resizer Lambda processes product images uploaded to S3 without content validation. An attacker can upload a polyglot file (valid JPEG header + embedded PHP/Python payload) or an image crafted to exploit ImageMagick CVEs (e.g., ImageTragick-style attacks). The Lambda runs outside VPC (CNAPP-AWS-EC-010) with unrestricted egress, enabling data exfiltration to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
mitigations: Validate image magic bytes and reject non-image content types
Deploy Lambda inside VPC with no internet access (use S3 VPC endpoint)
Process images using a restricted ImageMagick policy.xml that disables coders
Set Lambda memory limit and timeout to prevent resource exhaustion
Scan uploaded files with ClamAV Lambda layer before processing

threats.title: Stripe API key exposure via hardcoded credential
threats.description: The SARIF scan detected a hardcoded Stripe live secret key (sk_live_...) in application.properties (CWE-798). If the source code repository is compromised, leaked via CI logs, or exposed through the ErrorHandler stack trace disclosure (CWE-200), the attacker gains full Stripe API access. They can create charges, process refunds, access customer card fingerprints, and view complete transaction history across all merchant accounts.
threats.mitre_technique: T1552.001 -- Unsecured Credentials: Credentials in Files
mitigations: Move Stripe API key to AWS Secrets Manager immediately (P0)
Rotate the current Stripe key since it may already be in git history
Add pre-commit hook with gitleaks/trufflehog to prevent future secret commits
Enable Stripe restricted keys with minimum required permissions per service
Set up Stripe webhook signing to verify event authenticity

threats.title: Phishing via open redirect in auth service login flow
threats.description: CVE-2024-22262 redirect injection combined with the auth service login flow enables a convincing phishing attack. Attacker crafts a URL like https://acme.com/auth/login?redirect_uri=https://evil.com/harvest that passes initial domain validation due to the UriComponentsBuilder parsing flaw. After legitimate authentication, the user is redirected to the attacker-controlled domain with their authorization code, enabling account takeover.
mitigations: Upgrade Spring Framework to 6.1.6+ to patch CVE-2024-22262
Implement strict redirect_uri validation against pre-registered OAuth2 client URIs
Add DMARC, SPF, and DKIM for SES to prevent email domain spoofing
Display post-login redirect destination to user for confirmation
Log all redirect_uri values for anomaly detection

compound_attack_chains.name: SSRF-to-Account-Takeover
compound_attack_chains.chain: CVE-2024-22259 (WebhookHandler SSRF) -> IMDS credential theft -> AdministratorAccess IAM role -> Full AWS account control

compound_attack_chains.name: SQLi-to-Data-Breach
compound_attack_chains.chain: CVE-2024-1597 (JDBC SQLi) -> UNION SELECT -> payments schema -> cardholder data exfiltration

compound_attack_chains.name: Deserialization-to-Lateral-Movement
compound_attack_chains.chain: CWE-502 (OrderProcessor) -> RCE in Lambda -> RDS payments access -> cardholder data

top_3_priority_remediations: P0: Upgrade PostgreSQL JDBC to 42.7.3 (fixes CVE-2024-1597, risk score 25)
P0: Remove AdministratorAccess from ECS task IAM role (CNAPP-AWS-EC-002, risk score 20)
P0: Move Stripe API key to Secrets Manager and rotate (CWE-798, risk score 20)
